name: Spicy Cassava Fritters

ingredients.item: cassava roots, peeled, soaked, and grated (see here), or frozen grated cassava, thawed

method: In a large bowl, combine the grated cassava, salt, sugar, chili powder, scallion, and flour, and mix well.

Grab a pinch (about 1 tablespoon) of the paste and roll it between your palms to shape into an oval cocoon that is about 2 inches (5 cm) long and 1 inch (2.5 cm) thick in the middle. Repeat with remaining paste.

In a small saucepan or a wok, heat the oil over medium heat until it reaches 320°F (160°C) on a cooking thermometer. (You can also dip the pointy end of a wooden chopstick into the oil; if a steady stream of tiny bubbles appears around it, the oil is ready.)

Gently drop in the cassava cocoons and fry in batches, turning often, until golden brown (about 15 minutes). With a wire spider or slotted spoon, transfer fritters to a cooling rack or paper towel–lined plate to drain.
Serve within 1 hour after frying. You can store any leftovers in an airtight container and re-fry them whenever you are ready to serve.
